findings.evidence: `rg -n "theorem forall_legendreSym_neg_four_five_eq_one_iff|@\[simp\] theorem legendreSym_.*eq_one_iff" code/TauCeti/NumberTheory/Multiquadratic` shows this theorem at line 46 without an attribute, while the underlying one-factor normal forms such as `legendreSym_evenPrimeDiscriminant_neg_four_eq_one_iff` and `legendreSym_oddPrimeDiscriminant_eq_one_iff` are `@[simp]`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to this theorem if it does not create a loop; its RHS contains no occurrence of the concrete forall condition.
findings.issue: `forall_legendreSym_neg_four_five_eq_one_iff` rewrites the exported `[-4,5]` character condition to its intended normal form, but lacks `@[simp]`, so consumers cannot normalize the new public API with the existing simp-based Legendre interface.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "theorem forall_legendreSym_neg_four_neg_three_neg_seven_eq_one_iff|@\[simp\] theorem legendreSym_.*eq_one_iff" code/TauCeti/NumberTheory/Multiquadratic` shows the new theorem at line 71 without `@[simp]`, despite packaging existing simp-normal-form equivalences.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to this theorem as well, matching the one-factor Legendre normal forms it packages.
findings.issue: `forall_legendreSym_neg_four_neg_three_neg_seven_eq_one_iff` is the analogous normal-form lemma for the exported `[-4,-3,-7]` character condition, but also lacks `@[simp]`.

summary: The concrete character equivalences are the characteristic normal forms for the new exported prime-discriminant lists, but they are not registered for automation.
